posts_in_window.post_text: High motor pod vs low motor pod. Who doesn't like a little slow motion of props folding up and props coming out of the water ?

comments.author: Benedicto Espiritu
comments.text: Hopefully it weiighs way way less than an efoil which is 70 lbs

comments.author: Jimmy Jam Foils
comments.text: it’s like 1/3 the weight of an efoil

comments.author: Benedicto Espiritu
comments.text: Jimmy how much does everything weigh , board and everything ? Thanks

comments.author: Jimmy Jam Foils
comments.text: let me get back to you …good question. Loaded up for a sunrise session then a boat photoshoot with a team rider … hopefully get a chance to weigh tomorrow afternoon

comments.author: Aaron Pilon
comments.text: Fusion? Or Max? What do you recommend for downwinding, some eFoiling and small surf?

comments.author: Jimmy Jam Foils
comments.text: really loving the fusion. Does it all with extra power and run time. But is 8lbs heavier. But allows me to get across the lake or bay to chase down surf boats, cruise around etc. Max is a lot less run time … but if u wanna got light, and got a near by surf break … and not miles off the coast doing downwinders the max be nice and gets the job done. Need to do some downwinders next month when the gorge warms up and will have better feedback on downwinders but pretty sure fusion gonna get me the longer downwinder runs too. Now can leave from the hatch and power upwind

comments.author: Paul Schnetker
comments.text: I have a Fliteboard L2 that weighs about 40 lbs ready to ride. I weight 85kg (185 pounds) and I am intermediate rider. I am getting into Axis Wings. What front wing and rear wing would you suggest for an efoil in the surf? Thanks mate.

comments.author: Jimmy Jam Foils
comments.text: I was riding the spitfires the last few years with my lift efoil. But now loving the surge foils. Haven’t used em yet with my lift efoil but should be good

comments.author: Paul Schnetker
comments.text: cool thanks. I bought a 950 Surge and loving it with the adapter. I'm using a Fliteboard 245 rear wing and I have to go way up the front to handle the lifting. I am getting the axis rear wing adapter soon. Do you think the axis 320 surf skinny (I've heard they pair well with the Surge) is big enough or would you recommend something else for the back wing?
